config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary, because once you give society priority over the person too easily, it can become an excuse for abuse and injustice. From my perspective, a healthy society is built by protecting people’s dignity, freedom, and safety first, then balancing those rights with the common good when necessary. Social interests are important, but they should not automatically override the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kita justru menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi masyarakat” dipakai terlalu luas, hak minoritas dan kebebasan sipil sering jadi korban, jadi saya lebih percaya pada batas yang kuat terhadap kekuasaan kolektif. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to pressure people into silence or obedience. Society matters, but it is made up of individuals, so protecting rights like dignity, fairness, and freedom is not selfishness; it is the foundation of a healthy public life. When the state or the majority gets too much priority, minorities and ordinary people often pay the price first. For me, the better principle is balance: society’s interests matter, but they should never erase basic individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai bahwa kepentingan masyarakat bisa dengan mudah mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, penekanan kebebasan sipil, dan kerugian bagi minoritas. Kepentingan publik memang nyata, tetapi menurut saya ia harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja, seperti kebebasan berbicara, perlindungan hukum, dan martabat pribadi. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling siap menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak individu sambil tetap mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to pressure people to stay quiet, follow the majority, or accept unfair treatment, so I am very cautious of any idea that gives society automatic priority. Social interests matter, but they should be pursued through protecting individual dignity and basic rights, not by treating people as disposable. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices the person first; it is one that can balance public needs with firm limits on power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat bahwa ketika alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” diberi terlalu banyak ruang, kebebasan sipil dan hak minoritas sering jadi korban, dan itu bukan harga yang layak dibayar. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, perlindungan hukum, dan martabat pribadi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak individu sambil tetap mengejar tujuan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
